def: adj. (f. -ā), Void of qualities; having no distinguishing qualities, void of merit, worthless, bad; imperfect; barren.

knowledge; understanding; science
s.m. (f. -ā), A bird; a cloud; an arrow; the sun; the moon; a planet.

subs: or vihaṅg-var, 'King of birds,' or 'best of birds,' epithets of Garuḍ (the vehicle of Vishṇu).

s.m. Walking for pleasure or amusement, taking an airing, roving, roaming, wandering; — sporting, play, sport, recreation, &c. (i.q. bihār, q.v.); — a Buddhist or Jain temple or convent; a temple; a palace; — name of a district (see bihār); — the shoulder

def: s.m. The act of taking away, removing, taking; — going about for pleasure or exercise, taking a walk or airing, rambling, roaming; — relaxation, pastime, pleasure.
Vehari (a city in Vehari district, Punjab, Pakistan)
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Taking pleasure or relaxation, wandering or roaming about for pleasure, enjoying oneself; — sportive, frolicsome, gay; — beautiful, &c. (see bihārī).

v.n. To laugh gently, to smile, to laugh.

part. (f. -ā), Left, abandoned, deserted; desolate. lonely

def: adj. Foreign, strange (see vihīn; and cf. g̠arīb).

def: s.f. Hemp, &c. (i.q. bijayā, q.v.); — name of various plants; — a name of the goddess Durgā; — name of a festival in honour of Durgā (on the tenth day of the light half of the month Aśvin, when the image of Durgā is cast into the water).
s.m. 'The victorious,' an epithet of Indra.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Conquering, victorious, triumphant; — a conqueror.
adj. Mixed with the water of boiled rice, or with gruel (as sauce, &c.).

adj. (f. -ā), Knowing, intelligent; wise, learned; proficient, conversant, clever, experienced, skilful, prudent, discreet; — s.m. A wise man; a scholar, &c.

s.m. Distinguishing, perceiving, recognizing, discerning, understanding; distinction, perception, discernment, intelligence, knowledge, comprehension; science, learning, worldly knowledge of any kind (including all subjects except that understanding of the true nature of God or Brahma which is acquirable only by abstract meditation and the study of the Vedas); wisdom; — business, employment; — music

def: s.f. Learnedness; learning, intelligence (see vigyān).

s.f. Information, representation, respectful statement or communication; a report, an announcement.

s.m. One who knows or understands, an experienced person; a witness.

s.m. = S وجنتا विज्ञता vi-jṅatā, vulg. vigyatā, s.f. Intelligence, wisdom, learning, experience, readiness, skill, cleverness.
part. adj. Having known or perceived, having understood, having ascertained, having recognized.
adj. (f. -ā), Free from fever, or from pain; free from distress; — exempt from decay.

def: s.m. Falling or rushing (upon); attack (in battle), onset; engagement; — copulation.

def: s.m. Afternoon, close of day, twilight, evening, dusk.

adj. The sixtieth part of a kalā or of a degree, a second.

s.f. A perforated copper vessel which (when placed in a pan of water) marks the time by gradually filling; a sort of clepsydra or water-clock.

s.m. Alternation, alternative, option; — distinction; uncertainty, ambiguity; indecision, doubt, hesitation; — error, mistake, ignorance; — (in Gram.) admission of an option or alternative, allowing a rule to be observed or not, at pleasure; — (in Rhet.) antithesis.
adj. (f. -ā), = S وکمپت विकम्पित vi-kampit, part. (f. -ā), Trembling, tremulous, quivering; heaving, palpitating; agitated; unsteady.

def: s.m. (lit. 'having large ears'), Name of one of the Kuru princes on the side of Duryodhan, in the war against the Pāṇḍavas.
s.m. The district called Sārasvat, in the north-west of Hindūstān (generally considered to be a part of the Panjāb, and said to be so called as being 'the region from which Karṇ was excluded').
s.m. See vi-kāś, and bikās.

s.m. Becoming visible, appearance, display, manifestation, exhibition; — open or splendid appearance; — expanse, sky, heaven, ether; — a shining, blooming; — opening, expanding, expansion; budding, blowing (as a flower, or the heart; but in these senses better vikās); — pleasure, joy, happiness, enjoyment; — absence of manifestation or display; solitude, loneliness, privacy.
perf. part. of wikasnā, q.v. (and i.q. vikasit, q.v.). part. See vi-kāśit, and bikāsit.
adj. (f. -ikā), Causing to appear or shine forth, displaying; illustrating; illuminating; — causing to expand or bloom, opening, unfolding, spreading (i.q. vikāsak).
s.m. The act of displaying, display, manifestation, exhibition; — expansion, blowing (of a flower; — in this sense better vikāsan).
s.f. Appearance, display, manifestation, publicity; — expansion, diffusion.
adj. (f. -inī), Becoming visible or apparent, becoming manifested, shining forth; — expanding, spreading, developing, opening, budding, blowing (in these senses better vikāsī).
part. See vi-kāśit, and bikāsit.
part. (f. -ā), Made visible, displayed, exhibited; illumined; resplendent; radiant; — expanded, spread out, opened, blown, budded (in these senses better vikāsit).
part. (f. -ā), Expanded, opened, unfolded, budded, blown (as a flower); — rejoiced, cheered, delighted.
part. corr. of vikasit, q.v. part. See vi-kāśit, and bikāsit.
v.n. = بکسنا bikasnā, q.v. v.n. To open, expand; to blow (as a flower), bloom; to be pleased or delighted, become radiant with joy; to smile, laugh

def: adj. Large, great, &c. (i.q. bikaṭ, q.v.); — changed in form or appearance; distorted; — beautiful, pleasing; — obscure; — obsolete; — s.m. (S. vikaṭam), Danger, jeopardy; fright, terror, awe; — a boil, tumour.
s.m. Making known, declaring, announcing, publishing; — explaining, expounding; explanation, exposition; — avowing, acknowledging, confessing.
part. (f. -ā), Generally known, notorious, famous, celebrated, glorious; victorious (syn. mashhūr); known as, nominated, named, called; — avowed, confessed.

s.m. Stepping or going beyond, striding over or along; proceeding, going; — a step, stride, pace; — overcoming, overpowering; — great power or strength; — prowess, heroic valour, heroism; — name of a king (i.q. vikramāditya)

s.m. Throwing, casting, hurling, discharging; — projection; — throwing away; throwing about, scattering; sending, despatching; — dispersion, dissipation; — refutation (of an argument); — confusion, agitation, perplexity; craziness; alarm, fear; — (in Astron.) celestial latitude.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Shameless, unabashed; immodest, impudent (i.q. nilajj, q.v.).
adj. (f. -ā), Having a character different from what is usual or natural, strange, extraordinary; — bewildered, embarrassed; astonished, surprised; — abashed, ashamed.

adj. (f. -ā), Having different or opposite marks or characteristics; having distinguishing qualities or features; different, other, peculiar, strange, extraordinary; good; — s.m. A state or condition for which no cause can be assigned; vain or causeless state; — the act of distinguishing, perceiving, seeing, observing.

s.m. Play, sport, merriment, pastime, pleasure, delight, enjoyment (com. bilās); amorous pastime, diversion (with women, dancers, singers, &c.), dalliance, coquetry, affectation of coyness, wantonness; — grace, elegance, charm, beauty
subs: To sport, play, enjoy oneself, take pleasure, &c.

def: s.m. Flashing, gleaming; — sporting; — dallying; — play, sport, mirth.

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Playful, sportive, wanton, dallying, amorous; — addicted to pleasure, sensual, voluptuous; — a sporter, &c.; one who is fond of pleasure; a voluptuary, sensualist, rake; — fire; — the moon; — a snake; — an epithet of Śiva; of Vishṇu; of Krishṇ; and of Kām-dev.
s.f. (of next), A woman of pleasure, a coquettish woman; a wanton woman, a harlot; — a woman.
v.n. = بلسنا bilasnā, q.v. v.n. To be pleased or delighted; to derive pleasure (from), take delight (in), to enjoy (oneself).

v.n. = vilās karnā, q.v. s.m. Play, sport, merriment, pastime, pleasure, delight, enjoyment (com. bilās); amorous pastime, diversion (with women, dancers, singers, &c.), dalliance, coquetry, affectation of coyness, wantonness; — grace, elegance, charm, beauty

def: s.m. Liquefaction; dissolution, destruction, death; destruction of the world.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ولی (walī), from Arabic وَلِيّ (waliyy).

def: s.m. The act of dissolving, or liquefying; dissolution, liquefaction; destroying, destruction; corroding, eating away; removing, taking away; attenuating; (in Med.) an attenuant, escharotic.
tomato, love apple
wety: From وِلایَتی (vilāyatī, “foreign”) and بَین٘گَن (baiṅgan, “eggplant, aubergine”). Cognate with Bengali বিলাতী বেগুন (bilatī begun). Compare with Persian گُوْجِهفَرَنْگی (gowje-farangi, “European plum”) and Dari بادِنْجانِ رومِی (bādinjān-e rōmī, “Roman aubergine”).

def: adj. European, foreign; English; Persian; Turkish; Chinese, &c.; — of English manufacture, English-made; — s.m. A foreigner; — a native of Afghānistān; — an article of foreign manufacture; — a simpleton, a fool
more: adj. 1. Foreign; European; English, Persian, Turkish, Mogal, Chinese, etc. 2. English-made. valāëtī , n. m. 1. A foreigner. 2. A native of Afghanistan. 3. An article of foreign manufacture. 4. A fool. valāyatī baiṅgan . The tomato or love-apple; Solanum lycopersicum , Wat. valāyatī pānī , n. m. Soda-water. valāyatī seṅd. Euphorbia neriifolia (?). valāyatī mako ; Beng. tipārī . Cape gooseberry, or Brazil cherry.
adj. European, foreign. ولايتي خط wilāyat-ī ḵẖat̤t̤, A kind of Persian nastaﻌlīḳ character.
subs: s.m. The tomato or love-apple, Solanum lycopersicum
s.m. A kind of Persian nastaʻlīq hand
s.m. The Cape gooseberry, or Brazil cherry, Physalis peruviana.

def: Vile Parle (a suburb of Mumbai, Maharashtra, India)
ex: وِلے پارْلے کے ہِی نام پَر کَمْپَنِی کا نام بِھی پارْلے رَکھا گَیا
koronā vāiras: pārle jī kī rekārḍ faroxt, lāk ḍāun mẽ biskiṭ kampaniyõ kā bol bālā
The company was also named Parle after [the suburb of] Vile Parle itself.
wety: Borrowed from Marathi विले पार्ले (vile pārle).

ety: wilok˚ = S. विलोकय(ति), caus. of वि+लोक्

adj. Against the hair; against the grain; out of the usual or proper course; turned the wrong way, turned backward, reverse, inverted, inverse, contrary, opposite

s.f., or vilom-vidhi, s.m. Reverse action, doing anything in reverse order or backwards; — (in Arith.) the rule of inversion.

ety: wiloṛ˚ = S. विलोडय(ति), caus. of rt. वि+लुड्
def: v.t. = biloṛnā, q.v. (and cf. viloḍan). v.t. To stir, shake, agitate, to churn.
s.m. A sort of fish, Clupsa cultrata.
v.t. = wiloṛnā, or biloṛnā, q.v. v.t. = biloṛnā, q.v. (and cf. viloḍan). v.t. To stir, shake, agitate, to churn.
s.m. The wood-apple tree, and its fruit, Ӕgle marmelos (com. called bel, q.v.).
s.m. Name of a monkey-demon (slain by Balarām).
adj. (f. -ā), Free from dirt or impurity, pure, clean, spotless, stainless; undefiled, uncontaminated, immaculate; — white; transparent; beautiful

airport; airfield
wety: From Sanskrit विमान (vimāna, airplane) + तल (tala, base).

def: s.m. A by-road; a bad road; a wrong road (lit. or fig.), evil conduct, vicious course, immorality (i.q. vi-path, q.v.); — a broom, brush.

s.f. Difference of mind or opinion; disagreement, dissent; discrepancy, discordance; dislike, aversion.

adj. = بمبك bimbuk, q.v. adj. Red, fiery, flame-coloured.
part. (f. -ā), Unfaded; free from decay; refreshed, revived; fresh; — unsoiled; pure, clean.

s.m. Bewilderment; infatuation; temptation, allurement, seduction.
s.m. The act of confounding the mind and exciting the passions, tempting, alluring, seducing; temptation, seduction; infatuation, bewilderment.
adj. & s.m. Tempting, alluring, seductive, bewitching, fascinating; — a tempter, seducer, &c.
part. (f. -ā), Allured, beguiled, tempted, seduced; infatuated, bewitched; bereft of consciousness, bewildered, confounded, stupefied, besotted.

def: s.f. The Indian lute (i.q. bīn or bīnā, qq.v.; — the Vīṇā is supposed to have been invented by Nārad, the son of Brahmā, and has many varieties, which are enumerated according to the number of strings, &c.; its compass is said to be two octaves)
more: s. m. A bundle of thread, a skein.
subs: s.m. Name of one of the Gandharvs.

s.m. Loss; disappearance; perishing; — destroying, destruction; — name of the place where the river Sarasvatī is lost in the sand; — name of a country in the north-west of Delhi (said to be the same as Kuru-kshetr, or the country adjacent to the modern Paniput).

part. (f. -ā), Utterly lost; disappeared; perished; — destroyed, ruined; spoilt, corrupted.
s.f. Utter loss; disappearance; destruction, ruin.

def: s.m. Leading, guidance; training, discipline; refinement, gentlemanly bearing; good behaviour, propriety of conduct, decorum, decency; — modesty, affability, courtesy, humility, mildness; obeisance, reverence (cf. binay)

def: s.m. (f. -ikā), A remover (of obstacles); — a Buddh; — a name of the god Ganeś; and of Garuḍ (the bird and vehicle of Vishṇu); — a Guru or spiritual preceptor.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Well-trained, disciplined, &c. (i.q. vi-nīt, q.v.); — a well-behaved person; a modest person, &c.
s.f. The wife of Garuḍ; — the wife of a spiritual preceptor; — a spiritual preceptress.

s.m. Putting down, placing, laying down; depositing; entrusting; a deposit; — orderly arrangement, disposition, adjustment; — a site, or a receptacle (on or in which anything is placed or deposited); — collecting; collection, assemblage.

s.m. = وپکشي vi-pakshī, q.v. adj. Being on a differeut or opposite side, opposed, contrary (to), adverse, hostile, inimical; — s.m. An opponent, an adversary, a rival, an enemy; — a disputant; — (in Gram.) an exception; — (in Logic) a negative instance; an instance on the opposite side
subs: adj. Impartial, indifferent; — s.m. Impartiality, indifference.

def: s.f. The base of vipat, q.v.; and the form euphonically assumed by vipat in comp. before words beginning with u, g, &c., e.g. vipad-uddhār, vipad-grast, &c., for which see vipat.

s.m. Cooking thoroughly, dressing; ripening, maturing; — ripeness, maturity; — concoction (of food), digestion; change of state (in general); — unexpected consequence (of actions); unexpected event or occurrence, improbable result; — the consequence or result of any action (either in this or in a former birth) matured by the operation of time; — calamity, distress, poverty, difficulty, embarrassment; — flavour, taste.

adj. Being on a differeut or opposite side, opposed, contrary (to), adverse, hostile, inimical; — s.m. An opponent, an adversary, a rival, an enemy; — a disputant; — (in Gram.) an exception; — (in Logic) a negative instance; an instance on the opposite side

Turned the wrong way, reversed, inverted; inverse, converse, opposite, adverse, perverse, contrary, repugnant; unfavourable, inauspicious; — contrary to what is right; having a contrary disposition; changed for the worse, disordered; gone wrong or astray; — different (to, -ke), contrariwise (to); otherwise (than), differently (from, -se).
s.f. = S وپريتتو विपरीतत्व vi-parītava, s.m. Inversion; reverse or opposite state or condition; contrariness, contrariety, perverseness, &c.
s.m. = S وپريتتا विपरीतता vi-parītatā, s.f. Inversion; reverse or opposite state or condition; contrariness, contrariety, perverseness, &c.

s.m. Reverse; inversion; contrariety, perverseness, opposition, repugnance; enmity, hostility; — change, interchange, exchange; — change of purpose or conduct; — morbid change; reverse of fortune, calamity, misfortune, adverse fate; — error, misapprehension, mistake; failure of conception; — transgression, trespass.

r: vi-paryāy, i, pari, vi, vi-paryās, as, vi-paryay
def: (rt. i with pari and vi), s.m. = S وپرياس विपर्यास vi-paryās (rt. as with pari and vi), s.m. = وپريي vi-paryay, q.v.

s.f. The river Beas (one of the five rivers of the Panjāb; said to be so called as having destroyed the cord which the Muni Vasishṭha had tied round his neck to hang himself through grief for the death of his son slain by Viśvā-mitra; this river is identified with the Hyphasis of Arrian, the Greek name being a corruption of Bipasha).

s.f. The river Beas (one of the five rivers of the Panjāb; said to be so called as having destroyed the cord which the Muni Vasishṭha had tied round his neck to hang himself through grief for the death of his son slain by Viśvā-mitra; this river is identified with the Hyphasis of Arrian, the Greek name being a corruption of Bipasha).

s.m. Cause or occasion of trouble
or vipatti-yukt, adj. (f. -ā), Attended with misfortune, calamitous, unfortunate, unhappy.

ety: rt. pad with prati and vi
def: s.f. Going in different or opposite directions; perplexity, confusion; — difference, opposition (of opinion, or interests, &c.), mutual contrariety, contradiction, discrepancy; contest, dispute; conflict (of evidence); dissent, objection (in argument); — various acquirements, conversancy; mutual connexion or relation.
adj. Large, ample, great, extensive, broad, wide, spacious, roomy, capacious; abundant; deep, profound; — s.m. (f. -ā), A respectable man; — the mountain Meru; — the Himalaya range.
s.f. A respectable woman; — the earth.
s.f. = S وپلتو विपुलत्व vi-pulatva, s.m. Largeness, greatness, magnitude, extent, width, spaciousness.
s.m. = S وپلتا विपुलता vi-pulatā, s.f. Largeness, greatness, magnitude, extent, width, spaciousness.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Heroic, brave, valiant; mighty, powerful, strong; eminent, excellent; — a hero, &c. (i.q. bīr, q.v.); — an actor, a mime; — the heroic sentiment, heroism; — fire; sacrificial fire
subs: s.m. A distinguished hero; — a horse fit for the aśva-medh, sacrifice
s.m. Name of a king of Gūrjar; — a proper name (of men)
(lit. 'having an army of heroes'), s.m. Name of the father of Nala
s.f. Hero-worship. (For other compounds see bīr.)
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit वीर (vīra). Doublet of بیر (ber).

def: s.f. (m. vīra or vīr), The wife of a hero; — a heroine; — a wife and mother, a matron; — the plantain tree; — name of several plants.
s.m. Arranging, arrangement; — constructing; contriving, contrivance; making; compiling, composing; composition, compilation; — embellishing; embellishment; any artificial or ornamental fabric.

s.f. = vi-raćan, q.v. s.m. Arranging, arrangement; — constructing; contriving, contrivance; making; compiling, composing; composition, compilation; — embellishing; embellishment; any artificial or ornamental fabric.
part. (fem. -ā), Arranged; constructed; formed, made, prepared; contrived; effected; — written, compiled, composed; — put in, inlaid, set; — embellished, ornamented; furnished (with).
v.t. To arrange; to construct, contrive; to make, &c. (see viraćan, and biraćnā).

s.m. (dialec.) Fame, glory, &c. (i.q. birad, q.v.); — the dress of heroes; military uniform or accoutrements, the habiliments of war; — a badge betokening some excellence or superiority; — a badge of office (cf. wardī, and urdī).
s.m. Opposition; prevention; — vexation, annoyance; — a kind of Rākshas.
s.m. Absence of desire or passion, indifference (to), disinclination, aversion (to), disregard of all sensual enjoyment; stoicism.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Void of passion or desire; entertaining aversion or dislike; stoical; — an ascetic; a stoical person.
s.m. Separation, parting, absence, &c. (see birah); — pain arising from separation; — loneliness; distance; — relinquishment; abandonment, desertion; — cessation
subs: (˚ha+ag˚), s.f. 'The fire of separation,' the agony or disconsolateness of parted lovers
or virah-want, adj. (f. -vatī) = virahī, q.v. (For other compounds, see birah.)

def: s.m. = birhā.

adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Separated (from), absent, or apart (from another); lonely, solitary, deserted; — suffering the pangs of absence from a beloved one; love-sick; amorous; sensual; — one who is suffering the pangs of absence from a beloved one, &c. (see birahī, or birhī, the pop. forms).

adj. & s.m. = vi-rahī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Separated (from), absent, or apart (from another); lonely, solitary, deserted; — suffering the pangs of absence from a beloved one; love-sick; amorous; sensual; — one who is suffering the pangs of absence from a beloved one, &c. (see birahī, or birhī, the pop. forms).
s.f. = vi-rahiṇī, and birahan, qq.v. s.f. (of vi-rahī), A woman separated from her husband, or suffering the pangs of absence from her lover (i.q. virahin, and birahan, q.v.); — wages, hire.
s.f. (of vi-rahī), A woman separated from her husband, or suffering the pangs of absence from her lover (i.q. virahin, and birahan, q.v.); — wages, hire.

part. (f. -ā), Separated (from), retired or withdrawn from company; abandoned, deserted, forsaken, relinquished, left, left alone; solitary, lonely; — devoid (of), exempt (from), free (from); bereft (of), destitute (of).

ety: Prk. विरहियो, and विरहिआ; S. विरह+इकः, and इका
def: adj. & s.m. & f. Love-sick, &c. (i.q. virahī, and birahī and birhiyā).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Free from dust; — devoid of passion, passionless; — one who is free from passion.

s.m. Splendour, beauty; display; — a man of the regal or military class; — the first progeny of Brahmā; — (in the Vedānta phil.) the consciousness which perceives collections or aggregates; — the body.
s.f. A species of grass (i.q. dūrvā, or dūb); — name of a river.
adj. Shining, brilliant, splendid, handsome, gorgeous.
part. Shining, &c. (i.q. virājmān, q.v.).
